title: Mast Cell Crisis: Who Gets the Diagnosis?
description: Mast cell biology is among the most robustly established in immunology — the IgE-mediated degranulation cascade that drives anaphylaxis and allergic disease is not seriously contested. But the diagnostic criteria for Mast Cell Activation Syndrome, built around a single transient biomarker, are leaving a documented population of symptomatic patients in clinical limbo. This piece investigates whether the tryptase threshold serves patients — or protects institutions from diagnostic complexity — while holding the dissenting camp to the same evidentiary standard.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Mast Cell Crisis: Who Gets the Diagnosis?
